channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Massive fire breaks out at US Army warehouse in South Korea
item.description: A fire broke out at a renovated U.S. Army storage center in Busan, South Korea, Thursday night, U.S. Army Garrison Daegu confirmed to Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada will reduce immigration targets as Trudeau acknowledges his policy failed
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday that Canada will significantly reduce the number of new immigrants it allows into the country after acknowledging that his government failed to get the balance right coming out of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Without more action, it will be 'impossible' to meet Paris climate goal: UN
item.description: Without further actions to reduce planet-warming emissions, it will be “impossible” to meet the Paris Agreement goal of limiting global warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius (2.7 degrees Fahrenheit), the United Nations said in a new report Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: IDF kills Hamas terrorist it says worked for UNRWA, led charge on Reim bomb shelter massacre
item.description: The Israeli Defense Forces have 'eliminated' a Hamas commander not only involved in the horrific events of Oct. 7, 2023, but discovered to have been in the employ of the UN for over two years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgian government officials raid American think tank staff members' homes
item.description: Georgian government officials raided the homes of local staff of an American think tank who work on tracking Russian disinformation, raising alarm among prodemocracy activists, civil society groups and some U.S. officials ahead of Georgia’s Oct. 26 parliamentary elections.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Navalny widow to Americans: Don't take democracy for granted
item.description: Yulia Navalnaya, the widow of the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny, encouraged Americans not to take democracy for granted, in an interview on ABC’s “The View” that aired Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Mozambique's ruling party candidate declared winner of presidential election as rigging claims swirl
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's ruling party candidate Daniel Chapo was declared the winner of the country's presidential election Thursday as the opposition claimed the vote was rigged and one party said it would challenge the result in court.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentine police raid the Buenos Aires hotel where One Direction’s Liam Payne died
item.description: The police have raided the Buenos Aires hotel where ex-One Direction singer Liam Payne stayed before dying last week after falling from a third-floor balcony.
item.dc:creator: Almudena Calatrava, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Massive displacement from Israel-Hezbollah war transforms Beirut’s famed commercial street
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Inside what was once one of Beirut’s oldest and best-known cinemas, dozens of Lebanese, Palestinians and Syrians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah war spend their time following the news on their phones, cooking, chatting and walking around to pass the time.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Turkey hits US-allied Kurds in Syria, Iraq following terrorist attack on defense group
item.description: Turkey continued its strikes in Syria and Iraq for a second day following the terrorist attack on a defense company in Ankara, in what Turkey said were targeted hits on PKK terrorist locations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian lawmakers ratify pact with North Korea as US confirms that Pyongyang sent troops to Russia
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian lawmakers on Thursday ratified a pact with North Korea envisioning mutual military assistance, a move that comes as the U.S. confirmed the deployment of 3,000 North Korean troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Views of China hit new low: Survey
item.description: Americans have a deteriorating view of China and its relations with the U.S., according to a Thursday report published by the Chicago Council on Global Affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Train carrying 55 people derails on Norway's north coast, killing at least 1 person and injuring 4
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — At least one person was killed and four people injured when a train running along Norway’s northern coast derailed Thursday with 55 people on board, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Trash carried by a North Korean balloon fell on the presidential compound in central Seoul on Thursday for the second time, raising concerns about the vulnerability of key South Korean sites.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US announces $135M in humanitarian assistance to Palestinians
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken on Thursday announced $135 million in new humanitarian assistance for Palestinians and said American negotiators will meet with Israeli and Qatari counterparts to revive talks on a cease-fire despite uncertainty over Hamas’s participation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Tropical storm battering Philippines leaves at least 24 people dead in flooding and landslides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Widespread flooding and landslides set off by a tropical storm in the northeastern Philippines on Thursday left at least 24 people dead, swept away cars and prompted authorities to scramble for motorboats to rescue trapped villagers, some on roofs.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican troops kill 19 suspected cartel members, suffer no casualties: officials
item.description: Mexican army troops have reportedly killed 19 suspected cartel affiliates during a Monday shootout near Culiacan, the state capital of Sinaloa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's population has fallen by 10 million since Russia's invasion, UN says
item.description: The United Nations said Ukraine's population has decreased by around a quarter, or 10 million people, since Russia's invasion of the country in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah leader's expected successor killed in Israeli airstrike
item.description: Hezbollah on Wednesday confirmed one of its top officials, expected to be the group’s next leader, was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Explosion at Turkish aerospace facility ruled a terror attack
item.description: Turkish authorities say an explosion at a defense aerospace facility on Wednesday was a terrorist attack that killed an unknown number of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea has sent troops to Russia, Pentagon confirms
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin confirmed that North Korean troops have been transferred to Russia on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Group of metal detectorists uncover hoard of coins in England
item.description: A group of metal detectorists discovered a hoard of 21 coins near Okehampton Castle nearly a year ago, but the news of their finding was just recently unveiled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon chief: North Korean troops are in Russia
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin said North Korean troops appear to be in Russia. “We are seeing evidence that there are North Korean troops that have, that have gone to … Russia,” Austin told reporters in Rome on Wednesday. “What exactly they’re doing, left to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump campaign files FEC complaint over Labour staff campaigning for Harris
item.description: Former President Trump’s campaign team filed a complaint with the Federal Election Commission on Monday, labeling the U.K. Labour Party’s efforts to support Vice President Harris as “election interference.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman becomes stuck upside down between boulders for 7 hours while trying to retrieve dropped phone
item.description: A woman who became trapped in between two boulders in Australia after falling into a crevice while trying to retrieve her phone has been saved, NSW Ambulance says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ancient Roman statue discovered by construction workers in Bulgaria
item.description: During construction work in the city of Varna, workers found a well-preserved marble statue dating back to the Roman era outside the fortified walls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah naval base in Beirut as Iran rallies Gulf Arab nations
item.description: Hezbollah launched missiles on central Israel Tuesday. Meanwhile, Israel Defense Forces said it struck a Hezbollah naval base in Beirut, along with other terrorist targets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China holds live-fire drills near Taiwan, in another show of force
item.description: China's military is conducting live-fire drills around islands near Taiwan Tuesday that are being tracked by the Taiwan Defense Ministry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peru’s ex-president gets 20+ years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gunmen kill 7 people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Gunmen fatally shot at least seven people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir and injured at least five others, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar dumps heavy rain on Cuba as it heads toward the Bahamas
item.description: Its maximum sustained winds blew at 40 mph on Monday as Oscar moved west-northwest at 3 mph. The storm was located about 15 miles (25 kilometers) west-northwest of Guantánamo, Cuba, the U.S. National Hurricane Center in Miami said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reddit user discovers mysterious 'doorway' in Antarctica
item.description: "Monsters Inc." bedroom doors, and an underground bunker were more suggested explanations, some in jest others not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hezbollah vows a new phase in the war, as Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Sinwar
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah vowed to launch a new phase of fighting against invading Israeli troops Friday, a day after Israel said its forces in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza had killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, the chief architect of last year’s brutal attack on Israe
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9
